provenance: Independently hand-derived from the Harvey & Liu 2015 multiple-testing haircut (Decision D3, resolved 2026-05-22) using the Benjamini-Hochberg-YEKUTIELI (BHY) step-up. Harvey & Liu (2015), 'Backtesting', Journal of Portfolio Management 42(1), DOI 10.3905/jpm.2015.42.1.013, prescribe BHY for backtest/strategy selection because strategy test statistics are dependent; BHY is Benjamini, Hochberg & Yekutieli (2001), 'The Control of the False Discovery Rate in Multiple Testing under Dependency', Annals of Statistics 29(4), 1165-1188. BHY adds the Yekutieli arbitrary-dependence correction factor c(N) = sum_{j=1}^{N} 1/j (the N-th harmonic number) to the plain Benjamini-Hochberg 1995 step-up. Pipeline per trial i over N trials: (1) t_i = Sortino_i * sqrt(T_i); (2) one-sided p_i = 1 - Phi(t_i), standard-normal survival; (3) BHY step-up: sort p ascending, p_adj_(k) = min over j>=k of [ N*c(N)/j * p_(j) ], clamp to [0,1]; (4) selection: argmin p_adj_i, gated by p_adj_i <= q. Every t/p/p_adj value below was computed in Python (scipy.stats.norm.cdf for Phi, the BHY step-up by hand) — NO producer (autotuner) output is used. Re-pinned from the earlier plain-BH-1995 values per finding H1: BHY is the correct, more-conservative procedure (BH-1995 under-corrects by ~c(N)x). Tolerance for matching in tests: 1e-9 absolute (deterministic pipeline). Sortino and T inputs are scenario design choices, not producer values.
fdr_q_comment: Benjamini-Hochberg-Yekutieli false-discovery-rate level. Conventional 0.05. In the autotuner this must be a NAMED policy-dial constant (e.g. HARVEY_LIU_FDR_Q) with a sourced comment; the fixture pins q=0.05 only so the cleared-set assertions are reproducible.

bhy_dependency_factor.comment: c(N) = sum_{j=1}^{N} 1/j, the N-th harmonic number — the Yekutieli arbitrary-dependence correction. Benjamini & Yekutieli 2001. The autotuner must compute this (no magic literal): c_n = sum(1.0/j for j in range(1, n+1)).

bh_vs_bhy_discriminator.description: A 3-trial p-value vector that isolates the BHY c(N) factor: BHY's adjusted p-values equal plain-BH-1995's multiplied by exactly c(3)=1.8333... (where not clamped to 1.0). A RED test uses this to assert the implementation applies the harmonic-number factor, not plain BH.

mixed_one_clear_winner.description: Five trials spanning strong / mid / weak / marginal / negative skill. A_strong is the unambiguous BHY winner (smallest p_adj) and the ONLY trial that clears the q=0.05 gate under BHY. Note vs plain BH-1995: A_mid cleared under BH (p_adj 0.0220) but does NOT under BHY (p_adj 0.0503 > 0.05) — BHY is correctly more conservative. No raw p underflows to 0.0.

noise_only_none_clears.description: Six pure-noise trials: small both-sign Sortino values consistent with a zero-skill strategy's sampling variation. After the BHY step-up every p_adj saturates to 1.0 and NO trial clears q=0.05. This is the overfitting-protection property: the haircut rejects the ENTIRE noise set so the AI proposal falls through to the fallback/default cascade.

one_signal_among_noise.description: One genuinely skilled trial (C_signal, large t-stat) among five noise trials. Only C_signal clears q=0.05 under BHY and is the winner. Companion to noise_only_none_clears: proves the BHY gate is not so strict it rejects a real signal even with the c(N) inflation.
